steps.title: Set up the volume equation
steps.content:
The volume of a cylinder is $V = \pi r^2 h$. The first cylinder has volume $V_1 = \pi r^2 h$. The second cylinder has volume $V_2 = 392 V_1$, so:
$$\pi R^2 H = 392\pi r^2 h$$
Dividing both sides by $\pi$:
$$R^2 H = 392 r^2 h$$

steps.title: Backsolve the choices
steps.content:
Backsolving — testing choice B ($R = 8r$ and $H = 49h$):
$$R^2 H = (8r)^2(49h) = 64r^2 \cdot 49h = 3136r^2h$$
This is too large. Fails.

Backsolving — testing choice C ($R = 7r$ and $H = 8h$):
$$R^2 H = (7r)^2(8h) = 49r^2 \cdot 8h = 392r^2h$$
This matches exactly. That matches choice C — C is correct.
